contributor: university of maryland, college park, md
description: BALTIMORE AH AN IMMIGRATION PORT Its Past And Future Possibilities By BERTRAM N 3TUMP In the ancient archives of the Cus tom House may be found the first records of Immigration They begin with the year 1820 when on New Years Day the sloop Youngmans Companion from St Thomas W 1 landed two passengersWilliam Gal lup a citizen and F M Hormund the manifest reading A military man born In Germany Then February 15th a passenger ship the Hope of Philadelphia arrived from Liverpool bringing one American and 19 British subjects Again on February 19th there was a ship In from Amsterdam called Temper ance of Baltimore She brought 67 passengers one large German family Is noted viz Johann Adam Schaefer a farmer wife and nine children Unfortunately their final destination is not shown On July 4th the ship James from Bremen landed 55 German farmers The largest num ber of Immigrants during that year landed from the ship Massasolt from Amsterdam Her passengers came from Bavaria Baden England Ireland and France 116 in all The total Immigration for the year was about 2000 Only a few of tho passenger lists are In existence from 1821 to 1840 most of
